hypothesis: Incorporating reaction pathway and synthesis condition data from chemical literature into the continual pre-training corpus will enable LLMs to predict not only MOF physicochemical properties but also synthesizability scores, such that models pre-trained on synthesis-augmented corpora will achieve lower mean absolute error on property prediction tasks compared to models pre-trained solely on structural and abstract data, because synthesis context encodes implicit chemical compatibility constraints between metal nodes and organic linkers that are absent from MOFid string representations alone.
